## What is Blue Onyx?

Blue Onyx is a type of onyx that has a blue color. It can either be banded or unbanded. The properties of blue onyx depend on its type. There are two main types of blue onyx: natural and enhanced. Natural blue onyx is a semi-precious stone that is found in nature. 

Enhanced blue onyx is created when natural onyx is enhanced with dyes or other treatments. While both types of blue onyx have similar physical properties, enhanced blue onyx may have different metaphysical properties.

Blue Onyx is a member of the chalcedony family. It is found in many different locations around the world including Brazil, Uruguay, and the United States. The name “onyx” comes from the Greek word for “fingernail” because it was once used to make nail files.

Blue onyx has a waxy luster and a Moh’s hardness of 7. It has a trigonal crystal system and a vitreous to a pearly lustrous finish. Its color can range from light blue to dark blue, and it is often banded with white or grey stripes.

Blue onyx is believed to have calming and soothing properties. It is thought to help with decision-making, promote inner strength, and provide support during times of stress.

## Blue Onyx meaning

Blue Onyx meaning is defined as the power of protection. It is known to provide physical and emotional strength in times of need. It is the stone that promotes willpower, perseverance and self-discipline.

Blue Onyx also has a nickname, the Warrior Stone. This is because it gives people the strength they need to overcome any obstacle. 

The meaning of this stone also includes its ability to help one stay the course, even when things are tough. It will give you the courage you need to face your fears and overcome them. 

## Benefits of Blue Onyx 

Blue onyx is said to offer a number of benefits, both physical and metaphysical. Some of the most commonly cited benefits include:

– Helps to balance the chakras

– Promotes positive energy

– Can help to relieve stress and anxiety

– May improve circulation

– May boost immunity

– May aid in detoxification

Image by amazon.com

## Blue Onyx meaning and Chakra connection

– Helps with communication and self-expression

– Assists in finding your true voice

– Aids in the communication of needs and truths

– Facilitates creativity

– Connects you to your higher wisdom

Blue Onyx is connected to the Throat Chakra. Throat Chakra is located in the center of the neck at the level of Adam’s apple. It is connected to the element of Water and its color is blue. The Throat Chakra is responsible for communication, self-expression, and truth.

The Throat Chakra is out of balance when we have trouble communicating our needs or truths. We may be afraid to speak our minds or may hold back our creativity. When the Throat Chakra is balanced, we are able to express ourselves freely and creatively.

Blue Onyx’s connection with the throat chakra makes it a stone with many healing benefits and also a solution for many problems. It helps us to express ourselves clearly, find our true voice, and to communicate our needs and truths. Blue Onyx also helps us to be creative and to access our higher wisdom.

## Blue Onyx healing properties

The healing properties of blue onyx have been known for centuries. The stone is said to be effective in treating a wide range of ailments, from anxiety and depression to physical ailments such as headaches and stomach problems. 

Lets go through all the major areas that Blue Onyx covers in our lives.

## Blue Onyx emotional healing properties

– Blue Onyx is said to be helpful in overcoming fears and phobias.

– It is also said to be helpful in dealing with grief, loss and sadness.

– Blue Onyx is said to promote inner strength and courage.

Blue Onyx is the stone for soothing the mind and emotions. A cluttered mind is often the root cause of emotional imbalance and stress. The serenity of Blue Onyx can still the mind, bringing inner peace and emotional stability. 

It is excellent for those who are prone to anxiety or panic attacks, as well as those who tend to overthink things. By calming the mind, Blue Onyx also helps to ease insomnia and promote restful sleep.

Blue Onyx acts as that protective shield of the aura which bounces back the negative energies. It absorbs your negativities and transmutes them into positive ones. The stone is also useful when you want to get rid of your fear, phobias, and paranoia. It calms your mind and gives you the strength to face your fears.

Given the stone’s connection with mother earth, it sends negative vibrations into the earth to be grounded, and this is why it is also known as the “Worry Stone.”

## Blue Onyx physical healing properties

– Blue Onyx is believed to be helpful in the treatment of disorders of the bones and joints.

– It is also said to be helpful in the treatment of disorders of the nervous system.

– It is believed to be helpful in the healing of wounds and cuts.

– Treats skin disorders. 

– Helpful in the treatment of toothaches and gum infections.

While Blue Onyx is popularly known for its emotional and mental healing powers, this crystal possesses physical healing benefits as well which is now getting recognised by many healers.

This stone is believed to be helpful in treating disorders of bones, teeth, hearing and the eyes. It is also said to ease pains in the lower back and feet.

It helps in issues related to blood disorders and is also beneficial for regulating blood pressure and improving circulation. 

This crystal is also known to help in the detoxification of the body and in the treatment of disorders of the digestive system. As per many healers, Blue Onyx syncs with the body’s natural rhythm and helps in the absorption of vitamins and minerals from food.

## Blue Onyx metaphysical healing properties

– Blue Onyx is known as a stone of protection and strength.

– It is said to promote courage, stamina and self-discipline.

– Blue Onyx is also known to be helpful in the development of psychic abilities.

– It is said to be helpful in times of grief and sorrow, and to promote inner strength.

– Aiding in communication

– Helping you to achieve your goals

– Manifesting your desires

– Releasing negative emotions and thought patterns

– Connecting you with your higher self and spiritual guides

– Balancing the chakras

– Healing the body, mind, and spirit

Blue Onyx is a powerful stone that can help you achieve your goals and manifest your desires. It can also help you to release negative emotions and thought patterns. Blue Onyx connects you with your higher self and your spiritual guides. This process can help you to understand your true purpose in life. 

Apart from emotional and physical healing, Blue Onyx connects with the body chakra as well. It aligns and balances the chakras, allowing for a free flow of energy throughout the body. 

Blue Onyx specifically connects with the throat chakra that is located in the center of the neck. This chakra is responsible for communication, expression and creativity. When this chakra is balanced, we are able to express ourselves clearly and creatively. We are also able to communicate our needs and wants effectively.

If you are looking for a stone that can help you to manifest your desires, achieve your goals and heal your body, mind and spirit, Blue Onyx is the stone for you!

Being able to communicate effectively and expressing yourself truthfully plays an important part in addressing the mental and emotional needs of an individual. It allows for the free flow of communication which in turn helps to foster healthy relationships.

## How to best use Blue Onyx benefit 

Blue Onyx is a powerful crystal that deserves to be part of your daily life. There are many ways to use Blue Onyx but here are the best ones which give the maximum benefits:

## During meditation

Blue Onyx is the perfect crystal to use during meditation as it will help you to still your mind and connect with your inner wisdom. It will also enhance your concentration and focus. 

Given it is an excellent stone for soothing your mind and emotions, using Blue Onyx while meditation can benefit you at different levels.

All you have to do is, hold the crystal in your hand or keep it close to you while meditating. Feel the vibrations

of the crystal entering your body and filling you with a sense of calm and peace. Bring your attention to the stone and let it guide you into a deeper meditative state.

## For concentration and focus

If you are looking to improve your concentration and focus, Blue Onyx is the crystal for you. It will help to still your mind and give you the clarity and mental strength that you need to stay focused on your goals.

To use Blue Onyx for concentration and focus, keep it close to you while working or studying. You can also wear it as a necklace or bracelet. Another option is to place it on your desk or workstation.

## For decision-making

If you are struggling to make a decision, Blue Onyx can help. It will give you the clarity and confidence that you need to make the right choice.

To use Blue Onyx for decision-making, hold it in your hand while thinking about your options. Alternatively, you can place it on a table in front of you while making your decision. Let the crystal guide you towards the best option for you.

## As jewellery

Blue Onyx looks stunning when made into jewellery. Not only does it look beautiful, but it also has the added benefit of keeping the stone close to your body so that you can reap its benefits throughout the day.

To use Blue Onyx as jewellery, choose a piece that you love and wear it as often as you can. You can also give it as a gift to someone special in your life.

A Blue Onyx bracelet is my personal choice as it’s the perfect way to keep the crystal close to your body while still being able to show it off.

The other form of Blue Onyx jewellery that i highly recommend is the Ring!

Wearing a Blue Onyx ring is said to promote spiritual insights, intuition and psychic abilities. It is also said to help with decision-making, promoting wise choices and banishing bad habits. 

Blue Onyx is also known as a stone of protection, helping to protect the wearer from negative energies and influences. Wearing it as a ring makes it more personal and intimate, as well as a beautiful piece of jewellery.

## Best stone combinations with Blue Onyx

While there are many ways to combine different stones together, here are some of the best combinations with blue onyx:

Blue Onyx with Amethyst

This is a great combination for those who want to enhance their spiritual connection and intuition. Amethyst is a powerful stone that can help to open up the third eye chakra, while blue onyx can help to bring peace and calmness.

Blue onyx with Turquoise

The perfect combination for those who want to promote communication and self-expression. Turquoise encourages communication, while blue onyx can help to soothe the mind and emotions. Together they make a great team for opening up and expressing themselves.

Blue onyx with lapis lazuli

A great combination for those who want to boost their psychic ability and intuition. Lapis lazuli is a stone that is known for its ability to open up the third eye chakra, while blue onyx can help to calm and focus the mind. This combination is perfect for those who are looking to enhance their psychic abilities.

Apart from the above crystal combinations, Yellow-coloured crystals have a special affinity with blue onyx and can be used to intensify, expand and activate its energies. 

Crystals that can be used in this way include citrine, lemon quartz, golden beryl and yellow tourmaline.

Below are some other stones that go very well with blue onyx:

– Blue kyanite

– Blue lace agate

– Celestite

– Larimar

## Best ways to cleanse Blue Onyx

As we have seen above, Blue Onyx has many healing powers. But what happens when this crystal wades away all those negative energies from the wearer? 

Healing crystals like Blue Onyx absorb all the negativities. This is why they need to be cleansed and recharged from time to time to reignite their powers. Here are some ways to cleanse your crystal.

Using White light visualisation

This is one of the most popular ways to cleanse your crystals. To do this, simply hold your crystal in your hand and visualise it being bathed in white light. You can do this for as long or as short as you feel is necessary.

How this works is that the white light is said to be purifying and will cleanse your crystal of any negative energy it may have picked up.

Using Running water

This is another popular way to cleanse your crystals and can be done with either fresh or saltwater. Simply hold your crystal under running water for a few minutes, making sure that it comes into contact with all sides of the crystal.

Water is one of the purest elements which washes off any negative energy that may be attached to your crystal.

Cleaning with salt

This method is similar to the running water method, but instead of using fresh water, you use salt water. Simply add some salt to a bowl of water and stir until it is dissolved. Then, place your crystal in the bowl and leave it for a few hours (or overnight if you can).

The salt will absorb any negative energy from your crystal, leaving it feeling refreshed and cleansed.

Cleansing with sage

Sage is a powerful herb that has been used for centuries for its cleansing properties. To cleanse your crystal with sage, simply hold your crystal in your hand and waft the smoke from a burning sage stick over it.

The smoke from the sage will purify your crystal and remove any negative energy it may have picked up.

## Ways to recharge Blue Onyx

1. Through affirmations: 

This is a powerful way to recharge your crystal. some affirmations for Blue Onyx are:

-I am safe

-I am secure

-I am surrounded by love and protection

2. By sunlight: 

Sunlight is also a great way to cleanse and recharge your crystals. Simply place your Blue Onyx in direct sunlight for a few hours.

3. By moonlight: 

Placing your crystal under the light of the moon is also said to be a great way to cleanse and recharge it.

## What is the Blue onyx zodiac?

The blue onyx zodiac is Pisces. Pisces are known for their compassion, empathy, and intuition. They are also very creative and have a strong connection to the spiritual realm. 

Blue Onyx is a perfect stone for Pisces, as it helps to amplify their natural gifts and provides them with protection from negative energy.

Other important questions (Answered!):

What is the other name of Blue Onyx?

The other name of Blue Onyx is Lumerian Aquatine Calcite.

What is lemurian blue onyx meaning?

Lemurian blue onyx is said to be a stone of wisdom and peace. It is believed to promote communication and understanding, and to help one find their true purpose in life.

Lemurian blue onyx is helpful in relieving stress and anxiety, and to promote relaxation and restful sleep.

Is blue onyx dyed?
No, blue onyx is not dyed. It is a natural stone that forms in layers of white and blue.

Pink Agate is unique due to the combination of its colours. It is a soft, pastel pink with white bands running through it. The name agate is derived from the Greek word for ‘happy’.

## What is Pink Agate

Pink agate is part of the Agate family and is a semi-precious stone. It is a cryptocrystalline variety of chalcedony which is a microcrystalline quartz. The colours of pink agate can vary from pale pink to deep pink, and it is often banded or striped.

The name agate comes from the Achetes River in Sicily, where agates were first found.

Agates have been used since ancient times for their beauty and healing properties. In the Middle Ages, they were believed to ward off evil spirits and were used as talismans.

Today, pink agate is still prized for its beauty and is used in jewellery, as well as in home decor. It is also said to have many healing properties.

## Pink Agate Meaning

Pink Agate meaning stands for unconditional love, compassion, and healing. It is also known as the “Stone of Plenitude”. It brings good fortune, abundance, and fullness to life. Pink Agate is associated with the Heart Chakra. 

It balances yin-yang energies and helps to bring about emotional, physical, and mental equilibrium. It can help to dissipate anger and ease tension.

Being part of the Chalcedony family, Pink Agate gets its healing properties from Quartz. It has a long history of being used as a talisman and amulet to ward off danger and negative energy.

The Pink Agate meaning is one of calmness and reassurance. It is a stone that helps to ease anxiety and tension. It is also said to promote self-acceptance and self-love. Pink Agate is a gentle stone that can help to soothe emotions.

## History of Pink Agate

Pink Agate’s history goes back to the era of Neolithic man. It has been used as a talisman and amulet by many cultures throughout the ages. The Egyptians used it as a protection against evil spirits, while the Greeks and Romans used it as a talisman against misfortune. 

In the Middle Ages, it was used to ward off evil spirits and protect against the evil eye.

Pink Agate was also popular in the Victorian era. It was used in mourning jewellery and was thought to be a stone of comfort for those who had lost a loved one.

This gemstone has been used for ages and has always been in trend because of its unique beauty.

Different eras used Pink Agate in different ways. For example, in mediaeval times, it was used to ward off demons and evil spirits. It was also used as a protection against the “evil eye”.

During the Renaissance, agate was considered a stone of good luck and was often used in jewellery. It was also used as a talisman to ward off evil spirits.

Today, agate is still considered a stone of good luck. It is also said to have healing properties. It is believed to be helpful in treating stomach problems, skin problems, and headaches. It is also said to be helpful in improving mental clarity and concentration.

## What are the types of Pink Agate

1. Chalcedony Pink Agate: This type of agate is known for its ability to bring emotional stability and balance. It also promotes peace, love, and understanding.

2. Fire Agate: Fire agate is said to be a stone of protection, courage, and strength. It helps with clarity of thought and helps one to find their true path in life.

3. Moss agate: Moss agate is the stone of new beginnings. This stone brings abundance and prosperity to the wearer.

4. Pink Tourmaline: Pink tourmaline is the stone of love and compassion. It promotes self-love and self-acceptance.

## Benefits of Pink Agate

Pink Agate meaning is derived from the benefits it comes with. Below are the benefits this crystal possesses

-Aids in emotional healing

-Balances energy levels

-Boosts self-confidence

-Enhances creativity

-Fosters unconditional love

-Heals the heart

-Increases self-acceptance

-Promotes inner strength

## Physical properties of Pink Agate

Pink agate is a variety of chalcedony, which is a type of microcrystalline quartz. It is characterised by its pink colour, which is caused by the presence of iron oxide. It is a relatively soft stone, with a Moh’s hardness of 7.

Pink Agates have the structure of a layered cake, with bands of varying colours. The colours depend on what minerals are present in the layers. The most common colours are pink, white, and brown.

They have irregular, wavy bands and are often translucent. If you look at Pink Agate closely, you can see small crystals inside the stone. These crystals also known as inclusions are what give Pink Agate its characteristic appearance. 

## Pink Agate and its connection to chakra

Pink agate is a stone that is connected to the heart chakra. This chakra is located in the center of the chest and is associated with the color pink. The heart chakra is responsible for our ability to love and be loved. It is also associated with self-esteem, compassion, and emotional balance. 

Pink agate can help to open and heal the heart chakra, allowing us to feel more love and compassion for ourselves and others. It can also help to bring emotional balance and stability.

Those with an open heart chakra are said to be able to give and receive love easily, have strong self-confidence, stable emotions, and have a deep sense of peace. If your heart chakra is blocked, you may feel unlovable, unworthy of love, and have trouble giving or receiving love. 

You may also feel like you are carrying around a lot of emotional baggage or have difficulty letting go of the past. Pink agate can help to open and unblock the heart chakra so that you can start to experience more love and compassion in your life.

## Healing Benefits of Pink Agate

Pink agate is one of those magical crystals that seems to hold the promise of hope and healing. It has been used for centuries to calm and soothe the mind and body and is said to have numerous benefits and uses. 

Pink Agate meaning itself stands for self-confidence, grounding and an open heart to fill it with joy love and happiness. 

## Emotional healing properties of Pink Agate:

Pink Agate is a very helpful stone for emotional healing. It can help to soothe and calm emotions and to bring a sense of inner peace and harmony. It can also help to ease anxiety, stress and tension, and to promote a sense of calm and well-being.

Pink Agate is also said to be helpful for those who are struggling with grief, loss or heartbreak. It can help to ease the pain of these experiences and to bring comfort and healing.

When one feels low on self-confidence, wearing or carrying Pink Agate can be reassuring and uplifting. It can help to boost self-esteem and to encourage a positive outlook on life.

Pink Agate enhances relationships. By promoting communication, understanding and cooperation Pink agate helps strengthen the bonds of love and affection.

This stone of love and compassion opens the heart and promotes forgiveness, kindness and understanding. It can also help to ease the pain of heartache and push towards healing and recovery.

Like the ancient times, even now Pink Agate acts as a stone of protection. It shields the wearer from negative energies and promotes positive vibes. It protects against psychic attack and keeps the wearer safe from harm.

## Metaphysical healing benefits of Pink Agate

Pink Agate is a magical stone when it comes to the metaphysical benefits it offers. It has an immense calming energy, which can be very helpful in soothing anxiety, worry, stress, and fears. 

Pink Agate is also a powerful stone for restoring emotional balance and harmony. It can help you to feel more optimistic, positive, and hopeful. Additionally, Pink Agate is believed to promote self-love, self-acceptance, and self-esteem. It is also said to be helpful in attracting and maintaining romantic love.

Pink Agate drives away the negative energies of stress and anxiety and replaces them with positive, loving vibrations. It is also known as a “heart chakra stone” because of its ability to heal emotional wounds and traumas.

Pink Agate is also said to be helpful in promoting creativity, imagination, and mental clarity. It can also help to increase focus and concentration. Additionally, Pink Agate is believed to be helpful in strengthening memory and improving problem-solving skills.

## Physical healing benefits of Pink Agate

While Pink Agate is mostly known for its emotional and metaphysical healing properties, it also possesses physical healing benefits that help to improve overall health and well-being.

Pink Agate is said to be beneficial for the skin, hair, and nails and can help to improve circulation. It is also helpful in treating addictions and can be used as a detoxifier.

Some of the physical benefits of Pink Agate are:

– Helps to improve circulation

– said to be beneficial for the skin, hair, and nails

– helpful in treating addictions

– can be used as a detoxifier

## How to use Pink Agate

Pink agate is a beautiful and unique stone that can be used in many different ways. It has many benefits and uses, ranging from promoting emotional healing to helping with physical ailments. 

Pink Agate can be used in multiple ways to reap its benefits. Here is some effective and day-to-day use of this crystal.

As Jewellery:

Pink agate can be used as a beautiful and unique piece of jewellery. It can be worn as a necklace, bracelet, or even as earrings. Pink agate is a perfect way to add a touch of elegance to any outfit. The best form of Pink Agate jewellery can be found in the form of a necklace. 

A Pink Agate necklace can be found in many different designs. The most popular design is a Pink Agate pendant necklace. This type of necklace is very popular because it is very versatile. It can be worn with any type of outfit and it also has a very strong meaning behind it.

The bracelets are also very well known for their unique designs. They are often made with different types of beads and charms. The best thing about these bracelets is that they can be worn with any type of outfit.

Image by Flipkart.com

During Meditation: 

The process of using Pink Agate during meditation is pretty simple. First, choose a comfortable place to sit or lie down. Then, hold the Pink Agate in your hands or place it on your heart chakra. 

Now, close your eyes and focus on your breath. Inhale and exhale slowly. With each breath, try to feel the energy of the Pink Agate entering your body. Continue this process for 10-15 minutes.

As a Good Luck Charm: 

If you are looking for a good luck charm, then Pink Agate is the perfect choice for you. All you need to do is carry this crystal with you or keep it in your purse or wallet.

In the Bedroom: 

Pink Agate is also known as the ‘Love Stone. It is said to attract and maintain love. If you are facing problems in your love life, then place Pink Agate under your pillow before going to bed. It will help to attract love and improve your relationship.

## How to cleanse Pink Agate

Using water:

The simplest way to cleanse your Pink Agate is to use water. Simply hold your piece under running water for a minute or so, then dry it off with a soft cloth.

If your piece is very dirty, you can add mild soap to the water and gently scrub it with a soft brush before rinsing. Be sure to rinse all the soap off thoroughly though, as agate is porous and can absorb chemicals which might be harmful.

Using salt water:

Another popular method for cleansing Pink Agate is to use salt water. Simply mix together equal parts of salt and water, then submerge your piece in the solution for a few minutes. Rinse it off with fresh water and dry it off with a soft cloth afterwards.

Using Moonlight:

Moonlight is also said to be cleansing and energizing for Pink Agate, so leaving your piece out in the moonlight overnight is another option.

Cleansing your Pink Agate regularly will help to keep its energies strong and clear, and will also help to prevent it from absorbing negative energies from its environment.

Using Sage:

Sage is a common herb that is used for cleansing and purification. To use sage for cleansing your Pink Agate, simply light a sage stick and waft the smoke over your piece. You can also place your Pink Agate in a bowl of sage smoke if you prefer.

Virgos find comfort in pink agate’s practicality, and Capricorns are drawn to its disciplined and methodical energy.

• Is pink agate dyed? 

No, pink agate is not dyed. The color is natural and caused by the presence of minerals in the stone.

• Is pink agate natural ?

Yes, pink agate is a natural stone. It is formed from layers of chalcedony, a type of quartz, that has been dyed pink.

This crystal is also known as ‘anyolite’ and is an excellent source of positive energy and love. Two powerful stones combine to form this stunning crystal.

## What is Ruby Zoisite?

Ruby zoisite is a beautiful and unique gemstone that is perfect for jewellery making. It is made up of two different minerals, ruby and zoisite, which combine to create a stunning purple or pink colour. 

The red colour of the ruby is offset by the green of the zoisite, giving it a unique appearance that is perfect for making statement pieces of jewellery.

Ruby zoisite is a relatively hard gemstone, making it ideal for use in rings, earrings, and pendants. It is also a popular choice for carving and can be used to create beautiful sculptures and figurines.

Ruby zoisite is found in a number of different locations around the world, including Tanzania, Kenya, Madagascar, and India. It is a popular gemstone in Africa, where it is used in a number of traditional ceremonies and is believed to have a number of mystical properties.

## Ruby Zoisite Meaning

Ruby zoisite is a gemstone that is said to bring courage and strength. It is also said to help with emotional healing and promote creativity. When Ruby and Zoisite are found together in nature, they form a beautiful and unique gemstone. 

The combination of these two stones is said to create a powerful energy that can be used for healing and transformation. Ruby Zoisite meaning is derived from its two main components: Ruby and Zoisite.

Ruby:

Ruby is a powerful gemstone that has been used for centuries for its many healing properties. It is said to bring courage and strength and is also known as a stone of emotional healing. 

Ruby promotes creativity and is often used in crystal healing for its ability to help balance the chakras.

Zoisite:

Zoisite is a stone that is said to promote healing and transformation. It is the stone of creativity and is often used in crystal healing for its ability to help balance the chakras. Zoisite is believed to be a powerful stone for helping to release emotions and for promoting emotional healing.

When these two combine to form Ruby Zoisite, it creates a powerful energy that can be used for healing and transformation. ## How is Ruby Zoisite made?

Ruby zoisite is a gemstone that is known for its unique colours and patterns. It is a type of metamorphic rock that is formed when rocks are heated and pressured deep underground. 

The heat and pressure cause the minerals in the rocks to change and form new minerals. Ruby Zoisite is made up of a variety of minerals, including zoisite, ruby, and amphibole. 

The colours of Ruby Zoisite can range from green to pink to purple. The most common colour is green. Ruby Zoisite is found in many different countries, including Kenya, Tanzania, Madagascar, and India.

## History of Ruby Zoisite

Ruby Zoisite is a relatively new gemstone, first appearing on the market in the late 1960s. It is a composite stone, made up of green zoisite and red ruby. The two stones are often found in the same deposit and are often found in a swirl pattern when cut and polished.

The name “Ruby Zoisite” is a bit of a misnomer, as zoisite is actually the dominant stone in the composite. The ruby is often only a small amount of inclusions in the zoisite and is not always visible to the naked eye.

The first known deposits of Ruby Zoisite were found in Tanzania, and the stone is still most commonly associated with that country. However, Ruby Zoisite can now be found in a number of other locations, including Kenya, Madagascar, Namibia, and Pakistan.

Ruby Zoisite is not a particularly durable stone and is best suited for use in jewellery that will not see a lot of wear and tear. It is also not suitable for use in rings, as the stone is prone to chipping and scratching.

Ruby zoisite is a popular gemstone for use in making cabochons, beads, and other jewellery. It is also frequently used as a carving material.

Ruby Zoisite is also meant for those who are working on their crown chakra. It is said to help balance and open up this chakra. Crown chakra imbalances can manifest as issues with spirituality, intuition, and connection to the higher self. 

Ruby Zoisite heals these issues by providing a connection between the crown chakra and the heart chakra. This connection can help to bring about a more spiritual perspective on life and help to connect to one’s higher self.

Ruby Zoisite is a stone that works for the health of both the mind and the body. It balances the energies of the mind and the body. This crystal brings about a state of relaxation and well-being.

Ruby Zoisite is the stone of love, passion, and intimacy. It promotes positive and healthy relationships, and helps with emotional healing after a breakup or divorce. It is also a stone of fertility. 

Those in relationships will find that their bond is strengthened, and they are able to communicate better with their partner.

It can also help to rekindle the love and passion that may have been lost during tough times.

Ruby Zoisite is a powerful stone for health and vitality. It boosts the immune system and helps with cell regeneration. It is also said to be beneficial for the heart, lungs, and blood. Those recovering from injuries or surgery will find that Ruby Zoisite speeds up the healing process.

Ruby Zoisite meaning comes from the fact that this crystal can heal relationship issues, provide abundance and wealth while ensuring your emotional state is balanced well.

## What is Hematoid Quartz

Hematoid quartz is a type of quartz that contains inclusions of hematite, which gives the stone its red or orange colour. The hematite inclusions can be in the form of needles, plates, or spheres, and they can be scattered throughout the quartz or clustered together in one area. 

This crystal is also sometimes called “harlequin quartz” because of its multicoloured appearance. Hematoid quartz is found in a variety of locations around the world, including Brazil, Madagascar, Russia, and the United States. It is a relatively rare type of quartz, and it is prized for its unique appearance.

## Hematoid Quartz meaning

Hematoid Quartz meaning stands for its ability to bring hope in times of crisis. It is a powerful stone that helps to relieve stress, anxiety, and fear. It is also known as a lucky stone that brings good fortune.

This crystal is also known as a “bringer of peace” because of its ability to calm and soothe the mind and body. While Hematoid Quartz possesses many healing benefits, it also acts as a “grief stone” because it can help to ease the pain of loss. 

## History of Hematoid Quartz

Hematoid Quartz is a gemstone from ancient times. Its charm and beauty have made it a popular choice for jewellery and other decorative objects. The name “hematoid” comes from the Greek word for blood. This refers to the stone’s reddish hue, which is caused by the presence of iron oxide.

The ancient Egyptians used Hematoid Quartz in their jewellery and amulets. They believed that the stone had magical powers and could protect them from evil. The Egyptians also carved Hematoid Quartz into bowls and vases, which they used for drinking and storing water.

The Romans in olden times also valued Hematoid Quartz for its beauty and believed that it had healing properties. They used the stone to make bowls, vases, and other objects.

During the Middle Ages, Hematoid Quartz was believed to be a powerful talisman that could protect its owner from harm. It was also thought to bring good luck.

Hematoid Quartz is still popular today. It is used in jewellery, as a decorative object, and as a healing stone.

## Hematoid Quartz meaning and Chakra Connection

Hematoid quartz is a powerful stone that can help to balance and cleanse the chakras. It is particularly effective at clearing and balancing the root chakra and the sacral chakra.

While the below two chakras are closely related to Hematoid Quartz, this stone can also help to boost the energy of the other chakras and to promote a sense of well-being.

Root chakra

The root chakra is the first chakra and is located at the base of the spine. It is associated with the element of earth and with the feeling of safety and security. The root chakra is responsible for our sense of grounding and connection to the physical world. 

When this chakra is balanced, we feel safe, secure, and connected to the earth. When it is out of balance, we may feel anxious, disconnected, and unsafe.

Hematoid quartz is an excellent stone for balancing the root chakra. It can help to ground and connect us to the earth, and to provide a sense of safety and security.

Sacral Chakra

The sacral chakra is the second chakra and is located just below the navel. It is associated with the element of water and with the emotions of pleasure and creativity. This chakra is responsible for our ability to feel pleasure and to create and maintain healthy relationships. 

When this chakra is aligned, we feel confident and creative, and we are able to enjoy healthy sexual and emotional relationships. When it is out of balance, we may feel emotionally needy, sexually frustrated, and creatively blocked.

Hematoid Quartz helps in ensuring that Sacral Chakra is aligned and functional. The Sacral chakra is responsible for our ability to feel pleasure. It is important to have a balanced Sacral chakra in order to enjoy healthy sexual and emotional relationships. Hematoid Quartz can help to unblock and balance this chakra.

Hematoid Quartz or fire Quartz meaning is mostly defined by its immense healing powers. This crystal wades off the negative emotions away and keeps the thoughts balanced. As much as it is emotionally beneficial, Fire Quartz is also known for its physical and metaphysical healing benefits

## Other frequently asked questions:

• Black moonstone vs labradorite. Are they same?

No, black moonstones and labradorite are not the same. Black moonstone is a type of feldspar, while labradorite is a type of plagioclase. The key difference between black moonstone and labradorite is that black moonstone has a lower refractive index than labradorite. As a result, the black moonstone appears darker than the labradorite.

Larvikite and Black Moonstone are not the same. Black Moonstone is a type of feldspar, while Larvikite is a type of igneous rock. Though confused often, these two stones are different. The key difference is that Larvikite has a more fibrous or streaked appearance, while Black Moonstone is more smooth and glassy.

## What is Honey Calcite

Honey calcite gets its name from its colour, which is a deep golden yellow. It is a type of calcium carbonate, which is the main component of limestone.

Calcite is a very common mineral, but honey calcite is quite rare. It is found in only a few places in the world, including Mexico, Australia, and the United States. 

This crystal is also called golden calcite or yellow calcite.

Physical properties of Honey Calcite:

– Colour: Golden yellow

– Hardness on Moh’s scale: 3

– Crystal system: trigonal

– Refractive index: 1.658-1.662

Honey calcite is a translucent to transparent crystal with a vitreous to pearly lustre. It is usually found in massive form, but can also occur as prismatic crystals. The most common form of calcite, called calcite rhombohedron, has a rhombohedral shape.

## Ways to cleanse Honey Calcite

1. Using Water: 

You can cleanse your Honey Calcite by using water.  Hold your crystal under running water for a minute or two. You may use affirmations while this process. Turn the crystal on all sides to make sure water cleanses all sides of it. Post the wash, and use a soft cotton cloth to wipe the crystal off. The crystal is now ready to be used.

You can also cleanse your Honey Calcite using salt water.  Add some sea salt or Himalayan salt to a bowl of water. Stir it around to dissolve the salt. You can cleanse your Honey Calcite using smudging. Smudging is the process of using smoke from a burning plant, such as sage, to cleanse a space or object. Once the crystal is covered in smoke, you can allow it to air out or rinse it under running water.

You can cleanse your Honey Calcite by placing it outside in the moonlight. Leave it out overnight, and in the morning, bring it back inside. Doing this on a full moon day is recommended. 

## How to care for Honey Calcite

Honey calcite is a fairly soft stone, so it is important to take care when handling it. Avoid dropping or bumping it, as it may break or chip.

## How can you tell if a stone is Honey Calcite?

There are a few ways to tell if Honey Calcite is real. One way is to look at the colour. If it is a deep yellow or orange, it is most likely real. Another way is to look at the crystal structure. ## What is Jade Crystal

Jade crystal is a beautiful stone that has a deep meaning and history. It is a type of green gemstone that is often used in jewellery and other decorative items. It is typically a translucent green colour, but can also be white, yellow, or even purple. Jade is a popular choice for gemstone jewellery because it is durable and has a beautiful lustre.

The name “jade” comes from the Spanish word for “stone”. Jade has been used in art and jewellery for centuries, and its popularity has only increased in recent years.

Jade is made of two different minerals: jadeite and nephrite. Jadeite is the more rare and most valuable type of jade and is typically a brighter green colour. Nephrite jade is more common and is usually a darker green.

Jade is found all over the world, but the most high-quality jade comes from Myanmar (formerly Burma). Other notable sources of jade include Canada, China, Guatemala, Japan, Russia, and the United States.

## How is Jade Crystal Formed?

Jade crystal is formed when hot fluids flow through rocks and deposit minerals. Over time, these minerals slowly cool and harden to form jade.

## What are the Properties of Jade Crystal?

Jade crystal is a type of metamorphic rock, which means that it has been changed by heat and pressure. As a result, jade is very strong and durable. It is also resistant to scratching and chipping.

Jade crystal has a Mohs hardness of 6.5-7, which means that it can scratch other materials that are softer than itself, but will be scratched by materials that are harder.

Jade is a fairly dense material, with a specific gravity of 3.0-3.4. This means that it is slightly heavier than most other gemstones.

Jade crystal is typically a translucent green colour, but can also be white, yellow, or even purple. It has a vitreous (glass-like) lustre and a pearly to greasy shine.

Cherry Quartz is part of the broader Quartz family, which includes other popular stones such as Amethyst, Citrine, and Rose Quartz. Quartz is one of the most abundant minerals on Earth and is known for its hardness and durability.

Cherry Quartz meaning is defined by its vibrant red colour, which is said to represent energy, passion, and power. This type of quartz is also believed to promote creativity, motivation, and confidence. Additionally, cherry quartz is said to be helpful in attracting love and encouraging self-love.

This particular variety of quartz is relatively rare, and it is found mainly in Brazil. Cherry Quartz was first found in the early 1990s in Minas Gerais, Brazil. 

It is made by using cinnabar, which is a red mercury sulphide mineral, as a pigment. The quartz is melted and

mixed with the cinnabar, and then formed into crystals. The stone gets its beautiful red colour from the presence of cinnabar.

## Ways to cleanse Cherry Quartz crystal

## Using water

1. Fill a bowl with cool, clean water.

2. Add your Cherry Quartz crystal to the bowl.

3. Leave the crystal in the water for a few hours.

4. Remove the crystal from the water and allow it to air dry.

Alternatively, you may also use running water. Place the crystal in running water to get rid of all the impurities. Remove the crystal and wipe it off dry using soft cotton cloth.

## Using salt water

1. Fill a bowl with cool, clean water.

2. Add enough salt to the water to make it saturated.

3. Add your Cherry Quartz crystal to the bowl.

4. Leave the crystal in the salt water for a few hours.

5. Remove the crystal from the water and allow it to air dry or use a cotton cloth to pat it dry.

## Using Sunlight

1. Place your Cherry Quartz crystal in direct sunlight for a few hours.

2. Remove the crystal from the sunlight and allow it to air dry.

Make sure you do not leave the crystal in direct sunlight for longer durations as it may cause harm to the stone.

## Using moonlight

1. Place your Cherry Quartz crystal in direct moonlight for a few hours. Best is to do this during a full moon day in its direct moonlight.

2. Remove the crystal from the moonlight and allow it to air dry.

## Using Sage

1. Light a sage stick and allow the smoke to surround your Cherry Quartz crystal.

2. Allow the smoke to cleanse the crystal for a few minutes.

3. Extinguish the sage stick and allow the crystal to air dry.

## How to identify fake cherry quartz?

Cherry quartz is a type of quartz that is colored pink or red by inclusion of minerals such as hematite or goethite. It is found in Brazil, China, and the United States.

Fake cherry quartz can be distinguished from the real thing by its colour. Fake cherry quartz is often dyed or heat-treated to achieve its pink or red colour, whereas natural cherry quartz gets its colour from inclusions of minerals. Fake cherry quartz may also have a more uniform colour than natural cherry quartz, which can have streaks or areas of different colours. 

You can spot one other difference between fake and natural cherry quartz by looking at the edges of the crystals. Fake cherry quartz crystals are often cut or faceted, while natural cherry quartz crystals are usually jagged or uneven.

## How do you care for cherry quartz?

As with all gemstones, it is important to clean cherry quartz regularly to keep it looking its best. You can clean it with warm water and mild soap, or with a gentle jewellery cleaner. Avoid harsh chemicals and cleaners, as they can damage the stone. When not in use, store cherry quartz in a cool, dry place out of direct sunlight.

## What are the different types of cherry quartz?

The two most common types of cherry quartz are the African and Brazilian varieties. African cherry quartz is found in a range of colours from pale pink to deep red, while Brazilian cherry quartz is usually a brighter, more vibrant pink. There are many cherry quartz types and the most popular ones are as below

## Fire cherry quartz and its meaning

Fire cherry quartz is a type of cherry quartz that contains inclusions of hematite, giving it a distinctive red or orange hue. It is said to be a powerful stone for promoting motivation and courage. Fire Cherry quartz meaning can be helpful when manifesting your desires into reality.

## Volcano cherry quartz and its properties:

Volcano cherry quartz is a type of cherry quartz that is found in the mountainous regions of Brazil. It is a dark pink colour with red streaks and is said to be a powerful stone for promoting passion and sexuality.

## Raw cherry quartz

This type of cherry quartz is the most natural form of the gemstone and is typically a pale pink colour. It is formed by a process of natural crystallisation and is found in areas where there is a high concentration of silicon dioxide.

## Tumbled Cherry Quartz

Tumbled cherry quartz is a type of cherry quartz that has been polished through a tumbling process. This process gives the stone a smooth, shiny surface and brings out the natural colour of the stone. Tumbled cherry quartz is typically a pale pink colour.

## Faceted Cherry Quartz

Faceted cherry quartz is a type of cherry quartz that has been cut and polished into a specific shape. The most common shape for faceted cherry quartz is the round brilliant cut, which has 58 facets. Faceted cherry quartz is typically a pale pink colour.

## Cabochon Cherry Quartz

Cabochon cherry quartz is a type of cherry quartz that has been cut and polished into a specific shape with a domed top. The most common shape for cabochon cherry quartz is the oval, but it can also be found in the shape of a heart or a teardrop. Cabochon cherry quartz is typically a pale pink colour.

## Cherry Quartz vs other Quartz

• Cherry Quartz vs Rose Quartz

Cherry quartz is a type of quartz that is red in colour. It is named after its resemblance to the fruit of the same name. Rose quartz is a type of quartz that is pink in colour. It is named after its resemblance to the flower of the same name. Both cherry quartz and rose quartz are popular gemstones. However, there are some key differences between the two.

Cherry quartz is typically redder in colour than rose quartz. It also tends to be more transparent, making it more suitable for use in jewellery. Rose quartz is usually pinker in colour and less transparent. As a result, it is more often used for decorative purposes.

Cherry quartz is found in a variety of locations around the world, including Brazil, Madagascar, and the United States. Rose quartz is found in a variety of locations around the world, including Brazil, India, and South Africa.

• Cherry Quartz vs Strawberry Quartz

Cherry quartz and strawberry quartz are two different types of quartz. Cherry quartz is a type of quartz that is red in colour, while strawberry quartz is a type of quartz that is pink in colour. While Cherry Quartz gets its colour from inclusions of hematite, Strawberry Quartz gets its colour naturally. 

The main difference between Cherry Quartz and Strawberry Quartz is that Cherry Quartz is artificially coloured whereas Strawberry Quartz is naturally coloured. Secondly, Cherry Quartz is harder than Strawberry Quartz.

## What is the zodiac of Cherry Quartz?

Cherry Quartz is the stone for the Aquarians. Those with the Aquarius sun sign are known to be independent, humanitarian, and have a strong need for knowledge and freedom. They need a stone that can protect them from the negative energy of others and help them stay grounded. 

Cherry Quartz is perfect for this because it is a stone of protection and can help to deflect negative energy. It can also help to bring clarity and peace of mind, which is perfect for those who need to stay calm and focused.

## Green Opal meaning

The Green Opal meaning stands for a precious stone of great beauty. The name opal is thought to be derived from the Sanskrit word “Upala” meaning “valuable stone”. Opal is the national gemstone of Australia.

It is a stone with dual benefits. First, it is a stone of love, hope, and fidelity. The caring and nurturing energies of green opal help to create lasting relationships. Second, it is a stone of prosperity and good fortune. Green opal is said to bring abundance and fullness to one’s life.

Image by ebay.com

## What is Green opal crystal?

Green opal crystal is a type of opal that is green in colour. It is a relatively rare variety of opals and is prized for its unique colour. Green opal crystal is found in a variety of locations around the world, including Australia, Brazil, Mexico, and the United States. 

It is made of silica and chemically, it is hydrated silicon dioxide. They are slightly similar to quartz in their chemical structure but vary in the amount of water they contain.

## How is Green Opal crystal formed?

Green opal crystal is formed in a similar way to other types of opal. It begins as a silica-rich solution that seeps into cracks and crevices in rocks. Over time, the solution solidifies and forms the opal crystal. The colour of green opal crystal is thought to be caused by the presence of iron in the crystal structure.

## What are the benefits of Green Opal crystal?

Green opal crystal is believed to have a variety of benefits. These benefits include:

– Green opal crystal is thought to be helpful in balancing emotions and promoting calmness.

– It is also said to be helpful in relieving stress and tension.

– Green opal crystal is believed to be beneficial for the heart and circulation.

– Some people also believe that green opal crystal can help to ease digestive problems.

## History and origin of Green Opal crystal

Green Opal was first found in Peru in the early 20th century. It is thought to have derived its name from the Greek word “opalus”, meaning ” jewel “.

This particular type of opal is quite rare, and is only found in a few places around the world. The largest and most well-known deposit is located in the Andes Mountains of Peru.

Green Opal is a type of common opal that does not display the iridescent play of colour that is typical of other opals. Instead, it is characterised by its solid green colour.

The green colour of Green Opal is caused by the presence of trace amounts of chromium and vanadium. These elements substitute for silicon and oxygen in the opal’s structure, and absorb red and yellow light, resulting in the green colour.

This crystal is a relatively soft stone, with a hardness of 5.5-6.5 on the Mohs scale. It has a vitreous lustre and a specific gravity of 2.0-2.4.

Green Opal is not typically faceted, as its softness and lack of play-of-colour make it unsuitable for use in jewellery. It is most commonly used for carving and as a gemstone

It is said to be the chakra of love, compassion, and forgiveness. Green Opal activates the heart chakra and helps in the flow of energy and emotions. Druzy Quartz is known as the “Stone of Starlight”, and is said to bring the wearer good luck, abundance and prosperity. It is also said to promote creativity, inspire passion and bring clarity of thought.

## What is Druzy Quartz

Druzy quartz is a type of quartz that forms tiny crystals on the surface of another stone. The word “druzy” comes from the French word “druse”, which means “a crust of sugar”. It is a natural formation that can occur on any type of stone but is most commonly found on agates.

Druzy quartz has a sparkly, glittery appearance and is often used in jewellery and other decorative items. It is believed to have healing properties and is sometimes used in crystal therapy.

## History and Origin of Druzy Quartz?

Druzy Quartz is a type of quartz that forms naturally in a wide range of colours. It was first discovered in the Alps in the early 19th century, and since then has been found in many locations around the world. It is made up of tiny sparkling crystals that form on the surface of another quartz crystal. 

Druzy Quartz is believed to have healing properties and is often used in jewellery and other decorative items. In ancient times, Druzy Quartz was used as a talisman or amulet to protect the wearer from harm.

Druzy quartz can be found in a variety of colours, depending on the type of stone it is growing on. It is most commonly found in shades of white, silver, and grey, but can also be found in shades of pink, blue, and purple. In countries like Brazil and South America, Druzy Quartz is considered to be a national gemstone.

## What are the Physical Properties of Druzy Quartz?

Druzy quartz crystals have a vitreous lustre and are transparent to translucent. They have a Mohs hardness of 7 and a specific gravity of 2.65. Given the hardness of the crystal, it can be classified as a hard crystal compared to the others and does not require a lot of maintenance. Druzy crystals can easily go in the water as well. The lustre and quality are easy to be maintained.

Druzy quartz crystals typically occur as small, sparkly crystals on the surface of other rocks.

These are made of tiny quartz crystals that have grown on the surface of a rock. They are mostly silicon dioxide and look similar to quartz crystal clusters. Druzy quartz is white and clear mostly and looks sparkly and shiny.

Druzy quartz crystals have a high surface area-to-volume ratio, which makes them very reactive.

Druzy quartz absorbs a lot of negativity on a daily basis, so it’s important to cleanse it frequently. Below are some of the ways to cleanse it

## Yellow Calcite meaning

Yellow Calcite is a crystal that has multiple meanings. It impacts almost all facets of life. From ancient times, people have been calling it the ‘The great eliminator”. It is a crystal that drives away any dark or negative energy.

1. The first meaning of yellow calcite is that it is a stone of positivity and optimism. If you are feeling down or anxious, hold a piece of yellow calcite in your hand and it will help you to feel more positive and hopeful about the future.

2. Yellow calcite is also a stone of courage and strength. In difficult situations, yellow calcite can give you the courage and strength you need to get through it.

3. This stone is also called the stone for mental clarity and focus. When your mind is scattered or unfocused, yellow calcite can help you channel your thoughts and bring your focus back.

4. It also enhances the creative side of your mind. When feeling stuck creatively, yellow calcite can help you to break out of your creative rut and find new inspiration. Those into art, take a note!

5. Yellow calcite is also a stone of manifestation and abundance. If you are working on manifesting your dreams, yellow calcite helps you believe in it from within and attract what you desire.

6. Yellow calcite is also a stone of protection. If you are feeling vulnerable or exposed, yellow calcite can help you to feel more protected and safe.

## About Yellow Calcite and its history

Yellow calcite is a calcium carbonate mineral with a Mohs hardness of 3. It is insoluble in water and occurs in a variety of colours, including yellow, orange, red, blue, white, and colourless. It is found in a variety of geological settings, including sedimentary, metamorphic, and igneous rocks. It is found in regions like Europe, Australia, and North America.

Due to the prevalent presence of yellow calcite on earth, it has a long history. Yellow calcite has been used for centuries as a pigment and as a gemstone. It was first described in the early 19th century and has since been found in a variety of locations around the world. 

It is a type of calcium carbonate that was formed from the remains of marine organisms like corals and shells. For many generations, yellow calcite meaning has been associated with the sun, and it was used as a talisman to bring good luck and fortune. Not just the healing powers but yellow calcite has also been used in many architectures. The Sphinx of Giza is made out of Yellow Calcite.

## Yellow Calcite physical properties

Yellow calcite is opaque to clear crystal that is made of calcium carbonate mineral. It can be found in shades of yellow, ranging from light to dark. 

It has a Mohs hardness of 3 and a specific gravity of 2.71.

## Yellow Calcite meaning and chakra connection

## Emotional healing properties

1. Yellow calcite is a stone that is known to boost self-confidence and self-esteem. It is a stone that can help you to feel more positive about yourself and your abilities.

This stone gives you freedom from negativity. Instead of just walking away from it, Yellow Calcite helps you face it and move past it peacefully. You recover from it once for all and don’t shut it down or hide it in the closet. This defines the beauty of yellow calcite meaning.

4. Yellow calcite is also known to be a stone of mental clarity. If you are feeling scattered or confused, this is a great stone to help you to focus your thoughts and to find clarity of mind.

2. Yellow calcite helps in complete detoxification. It helps to cleanse and purify the body, mind, and spirit. If you are feeling sluggish or bogged down, this is a great stone to help you to release any negativity and to feel lighter and brighter.

3. Yellow calcite is also known to be beneficial for the digestion system. Having a strong and healthy gut helps in getting rid of most diseases. It can help to relieve any digestive issues such as nausea, vomiting, indigestion, and heartburn.

It makes you believe strongly and accept your goal. You begin to live your dreams even before achieving them. That’s when the manifestation starts and you get closer to your goal.

6. Calcite is a powerful energy amplifier and cleanser. The stone has a high vibration that will clear, open, and activate the chakras. It also helps to remove blockages and expand consciousness. Calcite is also good for clearing and amplifying energy in the home.

You may wear it as an amulet, necklace ring or anything that you prefer. A beautiful yellow calcite bracelet is a perfect way to use it.

Yellow calcite is also used for meditation. It is said to help in relaxation and opening up the chakras. When meditating with yellow calcite, it is advisable to keep the gemstone close to you. You may hold it in your hand or place it on your heart chakra.

## How to cleanse and recharge yellow calcite

Yellow Calcite is a powerful energy amplifier and cleanser. The stone has a high vibration that will clear, open, and activate the chakras. It also helps to remove blockages and expand consciousness. Yellow Calcite is also good for clearing and amplifying energy in the home.

There are many different ways to cleanse and charge Yellow calcite. Here are some of the most effective ways.

1. Using Water

One of the simplest and most common ways to cleanse calcite is by using water. You can either place the calcite under running water or soak it in a bowl of water. If you are using tap water, it is best to leave the calcite to soak for at least 30 minutes to allow the impurities to be removed. Remove the crystal now and wipe it dry with a soft cotton cloth. Using water is natural and keeps the crystal lustre intact.

2. Using Salt

Another effective way to cleanse calcite is by using salt. You can either place the calcite in a bowl of salt water or sprinkle salt on the calcite and then rinse it off with water.

3. Using Sage

Sage is a powerful cleansing herb that can be used to cleanse calcite. Simply hold the calcite in the smoke of a burning sage stick or bundle. You can also burn the sage and use your hands to bring the smoke closer to your crystal.

4. Using Sunlight

Sunlight is a great way to cleanse and charge calcite. Simply place the calcite in direct sunlight for at least 30 minutes. Make sure you do not expose it for a longer duration in direct sunlight.

5. Using Moonlight

Moonlight is also a great way to cleanse and charge calcite. Simply place the calcite in direct moonlight for at least 6 hours. Moonlight is considered the mildest way to cleanse your stone. It has a feminine energy that is soft and gentle and does no harm to the stone even if you place it for an extended duration.

## Crystal combinations to use with yellow calcite

In general, Yellow Calcite is a stone that can be used by anyone who wants to harness the power of the sun. It is a stone of optimism, hope, and happiness. It can help to dispel negative energy and to bring positive energy into your life.

## Comparison with other stones

## Yellow Calcite vs Honey Calcite

There are a few different types of calcite, but the two that are most commonly confused are yellow calcite and honey calcite. Both stones are yellow in colour, but honey calcite has a slight brown or orange tint to it. Yellow calcite has a bright, lemony yellow colour with no other hues. on the other hand, honey calcite is a bit more subdued in colour and has a warm, honey-like tone.

When it comes to calcite, the colour is not the only difference between these two types. Yellow calcite is a bit more fragile than honey calcite and is more prone to chipping and breaking. Honey calcite, on the other hand, is a bit more durable and can withstand more wear and tear.

If you’re looking for a stone that is durable and has a bright, lemony yellow colour, then yellow calcite is the stone for you. However, if you prefer a stone that is a bit more subdued in colour and has a warm, honey-like tone, then honey calcite may be a better choice.

## Yellow Calcite vs. Yellow Aventurine

There are a few key differences between yellow calcite and yellow aventurine. For one, yellow calcite is a calcium carbonate while yellow aventurine is a type of quartz. This means that yellow calcite is softer than yellow aventurine, with a Mohs hardness of 3 compared to 7 for aventurine.

Calcite is also more brittle than aventurine, meaning it is more likely to chip or break if dropped or handled roughly. Aventurine, on the other hand, is a bit tougher and less likely to show wear and tear.

Finally, calcite is typically less expensive than aventurine, making it a good choice for those on a budget.

## Yellow Calcite vs Citrine

The main difference between yellow calcite and citrine is that yellow calcite is a calcium carbonate mineral whereas citrine is a variety of quartz. Both yellow calcite and citrine are yellow-coloured minerals. However, the colour of yellow calcite can be anything from pale yellow to bright yellow, while the colour of citrine is always pale yellow to yellowish brown.

In terms of hardness, yellow calcite is 3 on the Mohs scale while citrine is 7 on the Mohs scale. Therefore, citrine is harder than yellow calcite.

When it comes to crystal structure, yellow calcite belongs to the trigonal system while citrine belongs to the hexagonal system.

It is also said to be helpful in treating conditions like arthritis and migraines. Yellow calc

## How to identify Yellow Calcite

Yellow calcite is a relatively soft and fragile stone, making it easy to scratch and chip. It is also a relatively dense stone, so it will feel heavier than other stones of a similar size. The best way to identify yellow calcite is by its colour; it is one of the few stones that are consistently and brightly yellow.

lava-stone.meaning_content:
Lava stone is an igneous rock that is formed from the solidification of lava. It is a type of volcanic rock that is characterised by its porous and vesicular texture. Apart from being this unique stone, the lava stone meaning comes from the many healing properties it possesses.

The name “lava stone” is derived from the Latin word “lavare”, which means “to wash”. This is because lava stone is often used in laundry and other cleaning applications.

Lava stone is composed of several minerals, including feldspar, quartz, and olivine. The most common type of lava stone is basalt, which is formed from the solidification of lava that has cooled relatively quickly.

Lava stone has a wide range of colours, including black, brown, red, and grey. The colour of lava stone depends on the type of lava that was used to create it. Lava stone crystal is often used in jewellery and other decorative items. It is also a popular material for making sculptures and other artworks.

## Lava Stone meaning

Lava stone is said to be a stone of strength and courage. It is also said to be a stone of rebirth and transformation. Lava stone is said to be helpful for those who are going through major life changes. It is also said to be helpful for those who are seeking to find their true purpose in life.

## Lava Stone history and origin

After a volcanic eruption, what’s left behind is lava stone. Lava stone is actually solidified lava and is found in a variety of colours, most commonly black. The stone texture is usually rough with a porous surface. Lava stone is found all over the world, with notable deposits in Hawaii, Iceland, and Italy.

Lava stone also known as Basalt, was first used by the Native Americans in tools and weapons. Lava stone has a long history of being used in jewellery, sculptures and other decorative items.

## How to cleanse Lava Stone

Lava stone is a very porous stone and can easily absorb negative energy. It is important to cleanse your lava stone regularly to remove any negative energy it has absorbed.

Using Water

One of the easiest ways to cleanse your lava stones is to simply rinse them under running water. You can also place them in a bowl of water and allow them to soak for a few minutes.

Using Salt

Another way to cleanse your lava stones is to place them in a bowl of salt. You can use sea salt, Himalayan salt, or any other type of salt. Allow the stones to soak in the salt for a few hours or overnight.

Using Incense

You can also cleanse your lava stones using incense smoke. Simply hold the stones in the smoke for a few minutes.

Using Moonlight

Using sunlight

You can also cleanse your lava stones using sunlight. Place your stones in direct sunlight for a few hours. Remove them from the sunlight and wipe it with a soft cotton cloth before using them.

After cleansing your lava stones, it is important to charge them before using them for healing purposes. You can charge your lava stones using any of the above methods.

## Hematite Rings meaning

Hematite Rings meaning is derived from the Greek word “haima” which means blood. The name was given to this stone because of its reddish colour. The colour of Hematite can range from a deep red to a blackish colour. The most common colour is silver-grey. The word “hematite” comes from the Greek word “haimatos” which means blood. 

Hematite is a stone that is believed to have many healing properties. It is said to be helpful in grounding and protecting the wearer. It is also said to be beneficial for the blood and circulatory system. Wearing a hematite ring can help to ground and protect the wearer, improve circulation and boost the immune system.

## What is Hematite ring?

A hematite ring is a kind of ring which is made of hematite, a kind of stone. Hematite is an iron oxide mineral. It is the most important iron ore. The colour of hematite is black or red. Its name comes from the Greek word “haima” which means blood. It is also known as the “bloodstone”.

Hematite rings are very popular because they are believed to have healing properties. They are said to be effective in treating blood disorders and in increasing the circulation of blood.

Image by etsy.com

## History and Origin of Hematite ring

Hematite rings have been used since ancient times. The first recorded use of hematite was in Egypt in the 12th century BC. It was used to make red and black pigments for paints and inks.

The first hematite rings were probably made by the Celts. They used hematite to make magical amulets and talismans. Hematite was also used in the Middle Ages to make signet rings. These were used to seal documents and to make impressions in wax.

Hematite rings became popular again in the 18th century. They were made as mourning rings to commemorate the death of a loved one. They were also given as gifts to new mothers to wish them good health.

The Victorians also wore hematite rings. They believed that the stone had the power to protect the wearer from evil.

Hematite rings are still popular today. They are worn as fashion accessories and as talismans.

## Hematite ring properties

• Hematite is a very dense stone. It is heavier than most other stones. It has a metallic lustre and it is an opaque stone.

• Hematite is found in a variety of colours including red, black, and silver. The most common color is black.

• Hematite is a very hard stone with a hardness of 6.5 to 7.5 on moh’s hardness scale.

• Hematite is a popular stone for making jewellery. It is often used to make rings, bracelets, and necklaces.

## Hematite ring breaking meaning

Hematite ring breaking is a unique scenario and could mean multiple things. 

If your hematite ring breaks, it could symbolise that it has done its job of grounding and protecting you. It could also mean that it is time for you to move on from whatever situation it was that you were using the ring to protect yourself from. 

Also, If your hematite ring breaks, it is important to listen to your intuition to determine what the meaning is for you.

It could also mean the hematite ring has absorbed too much negativity and needs to be cleansed. If this is the case, you can cleanse your ring by submerging it in salt water overnight. 

Refer to the section of how to cleanse hematite rings for more details.

## The correct finger to wear hematite ring in

There is no right or wrong. Hematite rings can be worn in any of the hands. 

That said, It is best to wear them in the left hand as it acts as a grounding force and helps activate the Root Chakra. It can also be worn in the right hand to improve circulation and boost the immune system.

## How to use hematite ring for healing

• For grounding and protection: Wear the hematite ring on your left hand. Next, imagine Roots growing from the soles of your feet, anchoring you to the Earth. Repeat this process alternate days for 5-10mins and you will notice a gradual change in yourself.

• For improved circulation and immunity: Wear the hematite ring on your right hand. When you sit for meditation, imagine a bright white light entering through the crown of your head and filling your entire body. It unblocks crown chakra and fills the body with energy and joy.

• For detoxification: Fill a bowl with warm water and add a few drops of lavender oil to it. Soak the hematite ring in it for 10-15 minutes. The detoxifying and grounding properties of hematite will remove negativity and toxins from your body.

• For stress relief and anxiety: Wear the hematite ring on your left hand. Take deep breaths and focus on your breath. Inhale and exhale slowly. As you exhale, imagine all the stress and anxiety leaving your body. Repeat this process for 5-10 minutes.

• For Courage, strength and inner peace: Wear the hematite ring on your right hand. Sit in a comfortable position and close your eyes. Imagine a bright white light entering your body through the crown chakra. This light fills your entire body with self-confidence, strength, and courage. It also calms your mind and gives you inner peace.

• For balance and emotional stability: Wear the hematite ring on your left hand. Close your eyes and take a few deep breaths. Focus on your breath and count each inhales and exhale. ## How to cleanse and recharge Hematite Rings?

There are multiple ways you can cleanse your hematite ring.

• Using Water:

One way to cleanse your hematite ring is to simply rinse it with water. You can do this by holding your ring under running water or by placing it in a bowl of water. Allow the ring to sit in the water for a few minutes before removing it.

• Using Salt Water:

Another way to cleanse your hematite ring is to submerge it in salt water overnight. To do this, you will need to Dissolve 1/4 cup of salt in 1 cup of water. Once the salt has dissolved, add your hematite ring to the water and allow it to soak overnight. In the morning, remove the ring from the water and rinse it off with clean water.

• Using Sage:

Sage is a common herb that is used for cleansing. To cleanse your hematite ring with sage, simply light a sage stick and hold your ring in the smoke for a few minutes. You can also place your ring on a plate of sage leaves and allow it to sit overnight. 

• Using Incense:

Incense is another common tool that is used for cleansing. To cleanse your hematite ring with incense, hold your ring in the smoke of a lit incense stick for a few minutes. You can also place your ring on a plate of incense ashes and allow it to sit overnight.

• Using other crystals:

You can also cleanse your hematite ring by placing it on a bed of other crystals. Some good crystals to use for this purpose are amethyst, quartz, or selenite. Simply place your ring on a plate or in a bowl of these crystals and allow it to sit overnight.

tree-agate-vs-moss-agate.meaning_content:
Tree Agate and Moss Agate belong to the family of Agate stones. Both these are beautiful stones but very different from each other. Tree agate and moss agate are both types of agate that contain tree- or moss-like inclusions. both have green patterns that resemble leaves or branches. 

They have a beautiful pattern on their surface which at times creates some confusion between the two stones. Many confuse them as one single stone while they are very different from each other. Hence many questions arise around Tree Agate vs Moss Agate and what are the key differences.

In the below topics, I have covered points on Tree Agate vs Moss Agate differences and how one can clearly distinguish between these two stones without any confusion. 

The main difference between tree agate and moss agate is that tree agate has a more defined tree-like pattern, while moss agate is more opaque and has a more undefined moss-like pattern.

The second main difference is the inclusions in these stones. They are also different. Tree agate typically has inclusions of quartz or chalcedony, while moss agate typically has inclusions of Hornblende, chlorite, or other minerals.

By just looking at the stones you can observe the differences in the pattern: tree agate has a more transparent, banded appearance with a tree-like pattern, while moss agate is more opaque with a moss-like or fern-like pattern.

While these are the main differences between tree agate vs moss agate, there are a few more important points to look at. Keep reading further to understand the key difference and learn in detail about tree agate and moss agate, their healing properties, zodiac signs and chakras.
